mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals an easing discount-rate regime, raising equity valuations and lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is confirmed when rate cuts persist for at least one monthly observation and fail if cuts occur alongside equity drawdowns.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are negative or below baseline in a majority of months where FEDFUNDS declines versus the prior month, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a short-run threshold marks weakening household income and future demand, which should pressure equities lower over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when unemployment accelerates rather than stays flat.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both rise together, it indicates improving nominal sales and payroll income, a joint demand-and-income impulse that supports S&P 500 returns over the next month. Either series alone is insufficient; the positive signal requires both to be rising.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-PAYEMS uptrend does not outperform each single-series condition in forward 20-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal has Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in INDPRO after a prior decline indicates improving industrial output and easing supply constraints, which should translate into positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails when production rises only because of temporary restocking without follow-through.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average after a prior 2-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if rebounds in INDPRO are followed by negative or below-baseline 60-day SPX returns in most triggered cases, or if the signal loses significance after one quarter.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real-rate pressure tightens financial conditions and depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism is strongest when inflation prints exceed the recent trend rather than merely matching it.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change > prior 6-month average and FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if inflation accelerations during high FEDFUNDS do not coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not materially stronger than each component alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in HOUST signals future residential demand, construction activity, and credit-sensitive growth, which should support equities over the next quarter. The effect fails when housing starts rise only after rates have already begun tightening sharply.
failure_condition: Reject if rising HOUST does not improve average 60-day SPX returns relative to baseline, or if the sign flips negative in periods of rapid monetary tightening.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable UNRATE combined with continued PAYEMS growth indicates resilient labor income and consumption, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal is invalid if unemployment is stable but payroll growth stalls.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 5.0% and PAYEMS rises month over month
failure_condition: Reject if triggered cases do not show positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the combined labor signal adds no predictive power beyond PAYEMS alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-tightening environment, a rebound in INDPRO paired with stable CPIAUCSL implies improving real activity without renewed inflation pressure, which should lift S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. The mechanism fails when output strengthens alongside reaccelerating inflation.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO rebounds with stable CPIAUCSL do not produce above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal turns negative when CPIAUCSL reaccelerates.
